meta.name: REPLACE Full Name
meta.reviewedBy: Initial automated draft - NOT yet clinician-reviewed
meta.patientFriendlyOneLiner: REPLACE one sentence a scared newly-diagnosed person would want to read first.

overview.molecular: REPLACE key genetics / biomarkers, if any

studies.intro: Curated list of published literature. Add every paper you find.

outcomes.intro: Published outcome data, presented as an outcomes study would. Use ranges across studies.

registry.intro: Individual Participant Data registry: one row per reported patient, pooled from every published report.
registry.dataStatusBanner: These rows are placeholders. Replace with curated, cited, real published patient data before treating any number as real.

fields.diseaseSpecificDeath: true if died of this cancer

treatments.intro: Educational summary only - not a recommendation. Decisions belong with a specialist MDT.
treatments.disclaimer: May be out of date and varies by country. Defer to your treating team.

emergingTreatments.intro: Newer / investigational approaches under study. NOT standard of care.
emergingTreatments.disclaimer: Investigational. Not a recommendation. Discuss with your team, ideally via a trial.

clinicalTrials.intro: How to find and join trials. Live links update automatically - prefer them over a static list.
howToEnroll: Ask your oncologist / a specialist centre which open trials you might fit.
Search the live links above (filter for 'recruiting') and note the trial ID + eligibility.
Have pathology, recent imaging, prior treatments and bloodwork ready.
Contact the study coordinator to confirm eligibility and ask about travel/costs.
Ask about expanded access / compassionate use if you don't qualify.
clinicalTrials.trialsNote: Static trial lists go stale; the live links are the reliable source. If you add specific trials, include registry ID, status and a verified link.

monitoring.intro: Suggested follow-up framework. Confirm your personal schedule with your team.

supportGroups.intro: Communities for this cancer (and broader networks that include it).
supportGroups.wanted: If you know of a group for this cancer, please add it.

centers.intro: Specialist centres. Not exhaustive or ranked; inclusion is not an endorsement.

questionsForOncologist.intro: Questions worth asking your team. Bring a written copy.
